title: corydalis kareliniana
intro: Corydalis kareliniana, also known as Karelin's Corydalis or Tian Kuan Cao, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae), native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine and tetrahydroberberine. The health benefits of Corydalis kareliniana include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into a tea or capsule, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
